pr.title: Added Fox item attr's and values
pr.author: AaronB-Hub

summary: Closed, unmerged PR that added and renamed item variable/attribute structs for Fox-related items, especially Fox laser and blaster, while also annotating light gun item attributes with inferred values. The main functional cleanup was replacing opaque FoxLaserVars fields like xDD4/xDD8/xDDC/xDE0 with semantic names scale, angle, speed, and pos, then updating itfoxlaser.c to use those names and a separate FoxLaserAttr for article special attributes. No PR body, comments, reviews, or reviewer feedback were present, so rationale and acceptance status are inferred only from the diff.
what_changed: Added FoxBlasterAttr in itCharItems.h with ten float fields and partial value/index comments such as x18 // [1] and x20 // [2].
Renamed FoxLaserVars fields from raw offset-style names to semantic runtime names: xDD4 became scale, xDD8 became angle, xDDC became speed, and xDE0 became pos.

Added FoxLaserAttr in itCharItems.h with fields including lifetime and scale plus several still-unknown xN float fields, with comments showing inferred data values such as lifetime // [35], scale // [3], and x24 // [1].
Added FoxIllusionAttr with two float fields and value comments, while preserving FoxIllusionVars layout with offset comments.

Reorganized and annotated light gun structs in itCommonItems.h: ItLGunVars, ItLGunAttr, ItLGunRayVars, ItLGunRayAttr, ItLGunBeamVars, and ItLGunBeamAttr now carry clearer variable names, offsets, and known attribute values.
Updated itfoxlaser.c to use FoxLaserAttr for article special attributes instead of treating article attributes as FoxLaserVars.
Updated Fox laser logic to use semantic fields in item->xDD4_itemVar.foxlaser, including scale growth, angle normalization, speed-based velocity, and pos storage.
Renamed it_8029C504's last parameter in itfoxlaser.c and itfoxlaser.h from arg9 to speed.
Removed the parameter name ref from the it_8029CCF8 prototype in itfoxlaser.h.
Removed ItCapsuleAttrs capsule from the Item xDD4_itemVar union in types.h, according to the provided diff excerpt.
Only changed a no-newline marker in itfoxblaster.c; there is no substantive code change shown there.
smart_moves: Separated persistent per-item laser variables from article special attributes: FoxLaserVars now represents runtime state at ip+DD4, while FoxLaserAttr represents x4_specialAttributes data.

Used observed behavior to name Fox laser fields: speed feeds cosf/sinf velocity, angle is normalized and mirrored, scale is clamped and applied via HSD_JObjSetScaleZ, and pos stores the spawn/current position.
Kept offset comments alongside semantic names, preserving layout auditability while improving readability.
Annotated attribute structs with known extracted values, making future decomp work easier even when field purposes remain unknown.
Propagated the speed name through the public header signature for it_8029C504, avoiding a mismatch between implementation evidence and declaration.
decomp_lessons: When a field's use is clear from arithmetic or engine calls, prefer a semantic name plus an offset comment over leaving xDD4-style names everywhere.

Do not conflate article special attributes with item variable storage; xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes should usually point to an attribute struct, while item->xDD4_itemVar.* is mutable runtime state.
Velocity calculations are strong naming evidence: a field multiplied by cosf(angle) and sinf(angle) is likely speed, and the trigonometric input is likely angle.
JObj scale setters provide useful evidence for naming scale fields, especially when the same field is clamped against an attribute maximum or target scale.
Value comments such as [35], [3], or [2/3 * pi] are useful breadcrumbs, but they should not be overinterpreted into final semantic names unless code usage supports them.
Header-only struct improvements should update all corresponding C-file field accesses in the same PR to keep the codebase searchable and compilable.
naming_conventions: Runtime item variable structs use names ending in Vars, such as FoxLaserVars, FoxBlasterVars, FoxIllusionVars, ItLGunRayVars, and ItLGunBeamVars.
Article/special-attribute structs use names ending in Attr, such as FoxLaserAttr, FoxBlasterAttr, FoxIllusionAttr, ItLGunAttr, ItLGunRayAttr, and ItLGunBeamAttr.
Known runtime fields can be renamed to short semantic names such as scale, angle, speed, pos, timer, lifetime, and max_scale.
Unknown fields remain x0/x4/x8-style inside attribute structs, while offset comments document byte positions.
Offset comments use ip+DD4 style for item variable storage and +0/+4 style for struct-relative attributes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff preserves layout through comments rather than changing field order for FoxLaserVars and light gun variable structs.

Renaming fields did not alter the underlying arithmetic pattern in itfoxlaser.c: speed * cosf(angle), speed * sinf(angle), atan2f-derived angle, scale clamping, and HSD_JObjSetScaleZ remain structurally the same.
A separate local FoxLaserAttr* attr was introduced before assigning item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, which documents the intended pointed-to type without changing the control flow shown.
The PR appears focused on type/name clarity rather than new matching tricks; there is no review or body text explaining a specific nonmatch fix.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided context.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so there is no evidence from this slice that the changes were accepted or merged.
Because there was no reviewer discussion, any reason for closure is unknown from the provided data.
searchable_terms: Added Fox item attr's and values
Fox laser scale angle speed pos
follow_up_queries: Check whether a later PR merged equivalent FoxLaserVars and FoxLaserAttr naming changes after PR 1457 was closed.
Search for current definitions of FoxLaserAttr, FoxBlasterAttr, and FoxIllusionAttr to see which field names survived.
Investigate why ItCapsuleAttrs capsule was removed from the Item xDD4_itemVar union and whether that change was intentional or reverted.
Compare light gun attribute naming in later commits to confirm whether ItLGunRayAttr and ItLGunBeamAttr value comments were retained.
Look for review or closure context outside this dump if determining why PR 1457 was not merged matters.
